fresh_facts.claim: US-led operations under 'Epic Fury' have struck over 11,000 Iranian targets in the past 30 days, including missile, drone, naval, and nuclear facilities.
Israel conducted strikes on over 12,000 Iranian sites, including the Caspian Sea naval base at Bandar Anzali on March 18, targeting warships, command post, shipyard, and port infrastructure.
An Israeli F-35I fighter jet downed an Iranian Yak-130 aircraft over Tehran during the conflict.
Israel conducted an airstrike on March 30-31 hitting the former US Embassy compound in Tehran, now an IRGC Basij base.
The IRGC suffered heavy losses including thousands killed and wounded at bases, with strikes targeting Tehran sites including Pasteur Street and National Security Council.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated on March 30-31 that Iran would fight regardless of US threats and doubted a ground invasion would occur.
US strikes exceeded 8,000 targets by March 21, including 130 vessels and the Natanz Nuclear Facility with bunker busters.
Iran struck multiple US military bases including Al Udeid, Ali Al Salem, Al Dhafra, Fifth Fleet HQ, and THAAD radars during the conflict.
President Trump stated on March 31 that US operations are expected to end in 2-3 weeks without necessarily reopening the Strait of Hormuz.
The Revolutionary Guard Corps issued a statement on March 31 threatening to target US business interests and companies in Gulf Cooperation Council countries starting April 1, 2026.
Mojtaba Khamenei, son of assassinated Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, was appointed as new supreme leader on March 8, 2026.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, an IRGC-aligned official, replaced killed official Ali Larijani on March 17, 2026.
Ali Larijani announced an Interim Leadership Council on March 1, 2026, accusing the US and Israel of destabilization efforts.
Iran continues to blockade the Strait of Hormuz, disrupting Gulf energy infrastructure amid sustained US and Israeli bombing campaigns.
European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen backed regime change in Iran, signaling a policy shift.
US Central Command warned Iranian civilians on March 8, 2026 amid reports of regime use of populated areas for military operations.
President Trump expressed involvement in Iranian succession dynamics on March 5, 2026.
Iran deployed missiles, drones including Shahed models, and conducted strikes with 1,000 kg+ payloads since March 10, 2026 as part of 'Operation True Promise IV'.
Iran threatened to seize Bahraini and Emirati territory and close the Bab al-Mandab strait with Houthi support.

US Central Command maintained high operational tempo asserting dominance over the Iranian navy, interdicting supply chains, and conducting precision strikes across air, land, sea, space, and cyber domains.
B-52 overland missions and attack helicopters in naval operations highlighted increased US air superiority over Iran.
Earlier US military deployments to the region included the USS Abraham Lincoln, USS Gerald R. Ford, and guided-missile destroyers.

regime_fall.current_status: Following Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's assassination and Mojtaba Khamenei's succession on March 8, 2026, the regime faces an unprecedented military onslaught with US-led operations exceeding 11,000 strikes and Israeli operations striking 12,000+ targets, while IRGC hardliners consolidate control amid deepening economic collapse from Kharg Island's destruction and Strait of Hormuz blockade. The regime continues retaliatory operations against US bases and threatens regional expansion, but faces severe internal succession instability and mounting casualties as European backing for regime change signals growing international isolation.
regime_fall.last_major_event: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen backed regime change in Iran on April 1, 2026, signaling a dramatic international policy shift that compounds the regime's isolation during its succession crisis and military collapse.
